heading: Definitions
label: 4302 Definitions
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this title”, meaning title II of , as added , , , known as the Foreign Missions Act, which is classified principally to this chapter. Amendments1994—Subsec. (a)(3) to (8). struck out par. (3) and redesignated former pars. (4) to (8) as (3) to (7), respectively. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “ ‘Director’ means the Director of the Office of Foreign Missions established pursuant to ;”.Pub. L. 103–236section 4303(a) of this title1987—Subsec. (a)(1)(G). added subpar. (G).Pub. L. 100–2041986—Subsec. (a)(4). amended par. (4) generally, substituting “which is involved in the diplomatic, consular, or other activities of, or which is substantially owned or effectively controlled by” for “involving diplomatic, consular, or other governmental activities of”.Pub. L. 99–5691985—Subsec. (a)(4). substituted “mission to or agency in” for “official mission to” in introductory provisions, and inserted “or which engages in some aspect of the conduct of the international affairs of such territory or political entity” before the comma at end of subpar. (B).Pub. L. 99–93

children.label: A
children.text: real property by purchase, lease, exchange, construction, or otherwise,

children.label: B
children.text: public services, including services relating to customs, importation, and utilities, and the processing of applications or requests relating to public services,

children.label: D
children.text: locally engaged staff on a temporary or regular basis,

“chancery” means the principal offices of a foreign mission used for diplomatic or related purposes, and annexes to such offices (including ancillary offices and support facilities), and includes the site and any building on such site which is used for such purposes;

children.label: B
children.text: an organization (other than an international organization, as defined in ) representing a territory or political entity which has been granted diplomatic or other official privileges and immunities under the laws of the United States or which engages in some aspect of the conduct of the international affairs of such territory or political entity,section 4309(b) of this title
“real property” includes any right, title, or interest in or to, or the beneficial use of, any real property in the United States, including any office or other building;
“sending State” means the foreign government, territory, or political entity represented by a foreign mission; and
“United States” means, when used in a geographic sense, the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the territories and possessions of the United States.

children.label: b
children.text: Determinations with respect to the meaning and applicability of the terms used in subsection (a) shall be committed to the discretion of the Secretary.
